assessor_1_raw: Retail escalation prediction: well-designed with temporal split, PR-AUC primary metric, and fair TF-IDF+LR baseline. Both models receive same text input. Per-agent and per-channel stratification are good confound controls. Agent ID ablation verifies design choice. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_2_raw: The design correctly uses PR-AUC for imbalanced binary classification. Chronological split with issue category and channel stratification is appropriate. Both models receive equal tuning effort (grid search with same budget). Bootstrap CIs at 95% confidence are included. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_3_raw: The escalation prediction experiment has appropriate controls for agent behavior confounding (tenure band, agent ID ablation, per-agent analysis). Queue policy change detection via label prevalence monitoring is a good deployment concern. Calibration curve is included. No methodological flaws. Verdict: defense_wins.
